title: Last One Standing
desc: A survival scenario quiz. Every choice matters. Choose wrong and you're dead. How far can you go?

title: Higher or Lower
desc: Populations, box office, streams, heights — guess if the next one's higher or lower. One wrong guess ends the streak.

title: Would You Rather Ranked
desc: Pick a side, see what the world chose. 30 brutal dilemmas ranked by real player votes.
Slide the tiles, merge the numbers, chase the 2048 tile before the board fills up.
credit: Original by Gabriele Cirulli · MIT License

title: Hextris
desc: A hexagonal spin on block-stacking. Rotate the core, match colors, survive the speed-up.
credit: Original by the Hextris Team · GPLv3 License

title: Clumsy Bird
desc: Tap to flap, dodge the pipes, and try not to faceplant on your very first attempt.
credit: Original by Ken Wheeler · GPLv3 License

title: Pac-Man
desc: Chomp every pellet, dodge four ghosts with minds of their own, and chase the high score.
credit: Original by Dale Harvey · WTFPL License

title: Ten by Ten
desc: Drop blocks onto a 10x10 grid, clear full rows and columns, and keep the board from filling up.
credit: Original by José Pedro Dias · No license declared, used with attribution

title: Galaxy Invaders
desc: A galaxian-style shooter. Wipe out the formation before they wipe out you.
credit: Original by Shekhar Patnaik · No license declared, used with attribution

title: Candy Match
desc: Swap and match colorful candies in rows and columns to rack up combos before you run out of moves.
credit: Original by Mia (MiaLearnsToCode) · No license declared, used with attribution

title: Brick Game
desc: Break every brick with a bouncing ball and a paddle you control. A classic arcade breakout.
credit: Original by Chan Meng · MIT License

title: Slime Volleyball
desc: Bounce the ball over the net and keep it off your side. First to the win condition takes the set.
credit: Original by Turing School / dtinianow · MIT License

title: Racer
desc: A pseudo-3D road racer. Weave through traffic, ride the hills, and don't spin out on the curves.
credit: Original by Jake Gordon · MIT License

title: Pong
desc: The original arcade classic. Mouse controls your paddle, AI controls the other. First to miss loses the rally.
credit: Original by Harsha Verma · No license declared, used with attribution

title: A Dark Room
desc: Stoke a fire, build a settlement, and uncover what's waiting outside in this text-driven survival adventure.
credit: Original by Doublespeak Games · MPL-2.0 License

title: Quantum Forge
desc: Tap the quantum core, automate an energy empire, and melt it all down for permanent Antimatter.

title: Neon Velocity
desc: An endless neon runner. Double-jump the rooftops, dodge the hazards, chase your high score.

title: Reflex Rush
desc: Five lights, one reaction. Tap the instant it turns green — jump early and you're disqualified. How fast are you, really?

title: Aim Rush
desc: 30 seconds on the range. Pop as many targets as you can before time runs out and track your accuracy.

title: Word Streak
desc: One five-letter word a day. Six guesses. Come back tomorrow and keep the streak alive.
credit: Original concept and implementation by Ajora Games

title: Solitaire
desc: The classic Klondike deal. Drag, tap, and clear all four foundations.
credit: Original by John Hatzimalis · MIT License

title: Sudoku
desc: Fill the 9x9 grid, break no rules. Three difficulties and a saved best time.
credit: Classic puzzle concept · original implementation by Ajora Games

title: Tetris
desc: Stack the falling blocks, clear the lines, and try not to top out.
credit: Original by Jake Gordon · MIT License

title: Chess
desc: Play white against an on-board AI. Adjustable search depth, undo and redo included.
credit: Original by Zhang Zeyu · MIT License

title: Snake
desc: The classic. Eat the pellets, grow longer, don't bite yourself or hit the wall.
credit: Classic arcade concept · original implementation by Ajora Games

title: Minesweeper
desc: Clear the grid without triggering a mine. Tap to reveal, flag what you suspect, trust the numbers.
credit: Classic puzzle concept · original implementation by Ajora Games
